title: Hierarchical ordered categorical priors branch for Driver/Race interactions
summary: High-cardinality identities are central here, and both CatBoost and sklearn target-encoding docs stress leakage-resistant ordered/cross-fitted encoding.
rationale: High-cardinality identities are central here, and both CatBoost and sklearn target-encoding docs stress leakage-resistant ordered/cross-fitted encoding. The current best pipeline mostly uses frequency encoding plus hand-built interactions, leaving richer driver-race-compound priors underexploited.
implementation_hint: Run a separate model branch using CatBoost native categoricals or manually cross-fitted target encodings with hierarchical shrinkage: `Driver`, `Race`, `Race_Year`, `Driver x Race`, `Driver x Compound`, `Race x Compound`, `Driver x Stint`. Use only fold-internal past data or internal cross-fitting; compare against current one-hot/frequency branch and blend OOF predictions.
expected_effect: Captures persistent strategic tendencies of drivers and event-specific norms without handcrafting many brittle rules.
risk: Easy to leak on repeated driver-race trajectories; gains may vanish unless folds are grouped by race or at least embargo adjacent sequence chunks.
